package router

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"net/http"
	"time"
)

// Positive scenario for bootstrap_builds_clients_inside_route_registration
// Anti-pattern: HTTP clients and other dependencies are built inside route handlers
// instead of being constructed at bootstrap and injected.
func SetupRouter(db *gorm.DB) *gin.Engine {
	r := gin.Default()

	// Anti-pattern: http.Client built inside route handler on every request
	r.GET("/users/:id/avatar", func(c *gin.Context) {
		id := c.Param("id")
		client := &http.Client{
			Timeout: 10 * time.Second,
		}
		resp, err := client.Get("https://cdn.example.com/avatars/" + id)
		if err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}
		defer resp.Body.Close()
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"avatar_url": "https://cdn.example.com/avatars/" + id})
	})

	// Anti-pattern: new http.Client built again in another handler
	r.GET("/users/:id/profile", func(c *gin.Context) {
		id := c.Param("id")
		client := &http.Client{
			Timeout: 5 * time.Second,
		}
		resp, err := client.Get("https://api.example.com/profiles/" + id)
		if err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}
		defer resp.Body.Close()
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"profile_id": id})
	})

	// Anti-pattern: DB session created inside handler instead of using injected db
	r.POST("/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		session := db.Session(&gorm.Session{PrepareStmt: true})
		var body struct {
			Name string `json:"name"`
		}
		if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&body); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}
		c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"name": body.Name})
		_ = session
	})

	return r
}
